Today, many of the products and furniture from the studio have become contemporary classics produced at a time when the Scandinavian design scene in general, and the Norweigan design scene in particular, was mostly appreciated for the furniture icons of its rich past.

Since 2010 Engesvik has successfully run his own studio Andreas Engesvik Oslo, focusing on long term collaborations with international brands such as Iittala, Muuto, FontanaArte, Asplund and Fogia. The studio works in a variety of design fields ranging from furniture and tableware to industrial design; among the studios more recent projects you will find such diverse projects as a toothbrush for Hay and an armchair for Swedish producer Fogia. Alongside the design practice, Engesvik has been dedicated to promoting Norweigan design as well as teaching and supporting younger design talents in order for them to reach out internationally. In 2012 Engesvik was appointed as Guest Professor at The University College of Arts, Crafts and Design (Konstfack) in Stockholm.
TAF Studio is a Stockholm-based design and architecture practice, founded by Gabriella Lenke and Mattias Ståhlbom, working to breathe new life into domestic products through refined design.

Born in New York in 1974. He graduated with honors in Industrial Design at the Pratt Institute in New York in 1996. After working more than 10 years as a senior designer at architecture firms in Copenhagen, Milan, Paris and London, he returned to New York where he founded his own studio.

His works concern different fields: from architecture to design objects for the home, consumer products, store planning and exhibition design. His projects, characterized by the lack of superfluous details and the influence of many cultures, have allowed him to receive several international prizes and awards. He has been working for Zanotta since 2002.

The HELLO chair, crafted with meticulous attention to detail, boasts a visually striking and emotionally appealing design. Its round organic shapes exude playfulness yet sophistication, while the deep and comfortable seat and backrest provide unparalleled comfort and support. Designed by Denys Sokolov, the HELLO chair transcends the ordinary. It is poised to become a sought-after collector's item that adds playful accents to any space, redefining interiors with its remarkable presence.

The concept behind our latest creation, the "Archipen" chair, celebrates movement and dynamism through its distinctive design. With its minimalist aesthetic, characterised by clean lines and flat legs twisted in various directions, the chair references the first kinetic work of the world-famous Ukrainian avant-garde sculptor and artist Olexander Archipenko, “Archipentura”.

A freelance designer based in Slovakia, Michal Riabic graduated from the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava, the capital city of the Slovak Republic, where he studied applied arts and graphic design. Riabic also spent time studying in Finland, no doubt developing his affinity for furniture design. Riabic is noted for his successful collaborations, most notably with Bene, the internationally renowned office furniture manufacturer, and TON, the acclaimed European furniture manufacturer known for their iconic bentwood furniture designs. Michal Riabic, now art director at TON, among other things, along with Tom Kelly, had a real hand in bringing TON into a more modern product offering yet one that would be at home with its traditional bentwood offerings. The goal is to link the products of the past and present together in a way that elevates them both. As Riabic himself related, new technologies come up all the time, and a designer must create for his time. In our time, people live and work at a pace not known before; they want a chair to be wider and more comfortable so they can sit back and relax. We build on a long tradition of bentwood furniture, but we still create things for today.

Isabel Ahm graduated as a furniture designer from the Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts in 2007. She has had her own design studio for more than ten years where she studies everyday life among the Danish population. The ways we eat, sleep and spend time together. Based on her studies, she designs furniture to meet individual needs and habits. Her design solutions address everyday challenges.

Rooted in design research, studies of social behaviour, and dreams of a more embracing world, Isabel Ahm's designs and products act as organisms in close contact with our everyday. By studying how people live and sensing what they dream of, Ahm creates objects that enrich lives more than just a material level.

Crafted with the best materials, each piece of furniture is designed with sustainability in mind to secure the future of our planet. Isabel Ahm strives to awaken the senses, nourish the soul and make us more present in the now and the world we live in. The aim is to fulfill a human need and bring functional comfort into our lives through reasoning and a well-thought-out concept.

The clear design language of the objects has integrity and a strong material understanding, allowing them to complement existing furniture and those yet to come. The objects depict the world Ahm envisions for her children - a world with kindness and thoughtfulness, creating more welcoming spaces for life's unfolding.

Ado Avdagić (Tuzla, 1970) was educated in Malta and worked and lived in Rome, America, and the Middle East. He is the founder of the internationally awarded multidisciplinary studio ‘Avado’, founded in 2008 in Tuzla. Shortly afterwards, he started cooperating with today's world-renowned brand, Artisan from Tešanj.

He continuously deals with product design for furniture, lighting, interiors, and creative project management. He thinks about the relationship between people, nature, and form in space, creating functional, ergonomic, and aesthetically convincing solutions that resist the passing of time. Ado Avdagić, the founder of multidisciplinary design studio ‘Avado’ established in 2008, works on various projects ranging from furniture, lighting, interiors, and creative direction. His design process is plan-driven.

He believes that the principles of natural design should be intertwined with innovative technology, which can ultimately be used to create a better world. His products have been featured in leading design magazines. He regularly exhibits at all the leading furniture, lighting, and interiors fairs in the world.

Founded in ’13, Simon Morasi Pipercic is a Zagreb-based design office that works on various projects ranging from furniture and spatial design to site-specific installations, art direction, and design consultancy. The distinctive identity of the studio lies in the intercultural space between elitism and egalitarianism, exclusivity and inclusiveness, avant-garde experiments and highly marketable products, which has helped the team to successfully build a specific position easily recognisable in the global professional context. The office has been granted with some of the most highly considered awards - German Design Award in 2017, Red Dot in ’13 and ’15 and Interior Innovation award in ’15. In ’14, they were a part of the team that designed Croatian pavilion at the Venice architecture biennale. Their work was published in several renowned magazines (Dezeen, Domus, Frame, Designboom etc.) and exhibited worldwide.

Raquel Pacchini is a Brazilian-born Italian designer, one of the new multipurpose designers in the world of communication and product design. Pacchini worked as a designer at Studio Urquiola before establishing it with Andrea Steidl. They work for a range of companies in the fields of art direction, graphic design, installations, and industrial product design.
Using a creative process based on aesthetic, product, and strategic research, it transforms the ideas and inspirations of its clients into value systems to give rise to a fresh perspective on the brand that revolves around the creative direction, products, and visual communication.

Zaven is a Venice-based design studio founded in 2008 by Enrica Cavarzan and Marco Zavagno. They operate at the crossroads of product design, graphic design, interior design, and art direction. For Zaven, design is an analytical process: each project is driven by extensive research taking form in the final product. Whether it is an object of everyday use or a visual communication project, Zaven always aims for formal clarity. The studio has initiated numerous collaborations with creatives from different contexts and holds classes and workshops in various universities, academies, and design schools. In the latest year, the projects of the studio have been exhibited in museums and exhibitions worldwide. Zaven boasts collaborations with institutions, often connected to the theme of visual communication, such as Ca’ Foscari University, Fondazione Sandretto Re Rebaudengo, La Biennale di Venezia, and the Nouveau Musée National de Monaco. Clients include Bitossi Ceramiche, CC-Tapis, Cedit, Kvadrat, Knoll, La Triennale di Milano, Luisa Delle Piane Gallery, Mercedes, MINI Bmw, Nike, Palazzo Grassi-PPinault Collection, TIM, Tod’s, Vero, Verso NY, Wonderglass, Zanotta, and Zucchetti-Kos.

Marta Ayala Herrera (Córdoba, España) is a graduate of Engineering in Industrial Design from Nebrija University and Istituto Europeo di Design. Currently based in Barcelona, she has worked in different fields such as product design, ephemeral architecture, and interior design. Her work focuses on the relationship of the individual with his environment through objects. She likes to experiment with industrial materials and craft processes to create her pieces. Finally, the main goal of her design practice is to look for and discover the new languages of contemporary design.

My work is focused on the relationship between humans and their environment through objects. I am always interested in exploring new materials and new ways of experiencing the world. I am excited to work in different areas and develop diverse projects.

"The hardest design is that of simple, honest products. To achieve this, you must use sober and really sensitive intelligence. Things evolve either from nothing or to nothing; at some point on this journey, you will find their essence, their true poetry. We should judge objects as a part of a full sensuous experience because design is what connects people with their own reality."

Matteo Zorzenoni was born in Treviso, Italy, in 1978. After attending the Art Institute, where he developed his interest in art and design, he studied at IED in Venice, graduatingwith a degree with a degree in Industrial Design in 2001.

The style that sets him apart and has made him one of the most promising Italian designers is the continuous study of the materials and being able to unlock their unexpected potential, like creating glasses made of concrete, glass structures, liquid metal tables, etc.
‍
His projects have been showcased in some of the most important exhibitions worldwide, including Maxxi in Rome, the Triennale di Milano, the London Design Festival, the Centre Pompidou, and the Biennale of Architecture in Venice.
‍
Zorzenoni has been a teacher at the University of Architecture of Venice (IUAV) and leads workshops and courses for IED in Madrid, the Domaine de Boisbuchet (Vitra Design Museum), Fabirca, Mexico Design Week, and Kozice.
‍
After a long experience as a consultant for Fabrica (since 2006), he currently works alongside Jaime Hayon, following the Italian branch of the studio.
‍
In parallel, he continues his research into the world of high-quality craftsmanship and the creation of new projects for clients such as Cappellini, Mercedes Benz, Replay, Alcantara, Benetton, Bosa, Miniforms, Somethingood, Agusta Westland, MM Lampadari, Torremato Nason Moretti, Novamobili, and others.

Born in Milan in 1977. In 2004, after a collaboration of almost 2 years with Enzo Mari, he started working with luthier Francesco Rivolta. In 2007 he opened his own studio in Milan. From 2009 to 2015 he was a consultant at Michele De Lucchi's studio. Since 2016 he has directed a product design course at the Free University of Bolzano, as well as teaching at other Italian and foreign universities. Italian Fellow at the prestigious American Academy in Rome in 2013. Studio Francesco Faccin regularly collaborates with Italian and international clients such as design galleries, private companies, public institutions, NGOs. His work has been published in the most important worldwide magazines.

Christophe de la Fontaine is co-founder and owner of Dante – Goods and Bads, GmbH a German based furniture and home accessory brand with international reputation, with his partner, contemporary artist Aylin Langreuter. Starting his education as a sculptor in Luxemburg he completed his studies with a degree in industrial design from ABK-Stuttgart, taught by Richard Sapper. After a journey at London based Therefore-Product Design Consultant and Lissoni Associati in Milan, he headed the design department at the studio of Patricia Urquiola until 2010. Working independently as a product designer ever since he has received numerous international awards, including the red dot award, the Good Design award and the iF GOLD DESIGN AWARD while being listed by Wallpaper* among the world’s 100 best designers. Since 2018 he is professor for industrial design at the state academy of fine arts in Stuttgart.

Andrea Steidl graduated from Politecnico di Milano with a degree in industrial design and began his career immediately at Patricia Urquiola’s studio. He went on to become a creative manager and remained with the firm until 2016. He was responsible for the design and product areas and oversaw limited editions, temporary installations, site-specific projects, and interior design for a range of clients.

At the same time, in academic circles, he started working both as an assistant professor at the Politecnico di Milano and as a course supervisor at Domus Academy and the Scuola Politecnica di Design.

Zoran Jedrejcic is a Croatian designer and art director from Split with an Italian background. After seven years of fruitful cooperation with Ettore Sottsass, he opened a design studio in Milan and, more recently, one in Belgrade. He has designed for many international brands.

He won awards such as the Red Dot and IF Design Awards, and his works have been exhibited in prestigious museums such as MAK Vienna, ICA Boston, MoMA NY, and Ozone Centre Tokyo, among others. Parallel to his profession, he has been a visiting professor at ISIA-Florence, NABA-Milano, IAAD-Torino, and the Academy of Fine Arts-Belgrade. Currently, he is the Art Director of the Belgrade Furniture Fair and several regional design brands.

Rainer Bachschmid was born in the south of Germany at the lake Constance. Among his training as a cabinetmaker, he studied industrial design at the University of Wuppertal. After graduation in 1994, he collaborated with some designers, like Babel Design and Moll Design. Following Rainer Bachschmid, he moved to Switzerland.

He worked within 10 years as the head of development and design for the furniture factory Reinhard AG. Since 2006, he has worked at his own design studio, rabadesign, in Switzerland.

Salih Teskeredzic obtained a BA at the Architectural Faculty in Sarajevo in 1987 and an MA in Product Design at the Academy of Fine Arts in Sarajevo in 2003. University professor Salih Teskeredzic has taught industrial design since 2004, and since 2006 he has been the Head of Product Design Department at the Academy of Fine Arts in Sarajevo and a visiting professor at the Polytechnic Faculty in Bihac and the Civil Engineering Faculty in Sarajevo.

In 2000, he founded his first design and architecture studio in Vienna, and in 2007, he founded AD ARTE studio in Sarajevo. The main scope of the studio's work is dedicated to product design, interior design, and architecture. His works have been produced in several European countries: Austria, Denmark, Italy, and Germany, as well as Bosnia and Herzegovina.

He exhibited at several fairs, including Milan, Cologne, Paris, Copenhagen, Vienna, Ulm, Zagreb, Belgrade, and Sarajevo, as well as in the solo exhibitions Design+Architektur in Vienna in 1999, Sarajevo in 1997, and 2001.

Mads K. Johansen was a passionate and innovative furniture designer.With his background as a cabinet maker, Mads was able to appreciate and recognize the strengths and possibilities of every single piece of wood.

His knowledge and creativity resulted in technical innovations and beautiful details throughout his entire career.

In 2014, he passed away after a long period of serious illness, but his furnitures lives on.

Creation is an uncontrolled muscle," according to Arik Levy (born 1963).Artist, technician, photographer, designer, and video artist, Levy's skills are multi-disciplinary, and his works are shown and installed worldwide. Best known publicly for his sculptures, such as his signature Rock pieces, his installations, limited editions, and design, Levy nevertheless feels "the world is about people, not objects."

Hailing originally from Israel and moving to Europe after his first participation in a group sculpture exhibition in Tel-Aviv in 1986, Levy set up his studio in Paris in 1992. He was heavily dyslexic; art became his medium of expression. His formation was unconventional, where surfing and painting on surfboards as if they were canvas, as well as his art and graphic design studio, took all of his time back home. After years of practice, Levy decides to take on studies at the Art Center Europe in Switzerland, where he earns a BS with distinction 1991.

After a stint in Japan where he consolidates his ideas, observing Japanese minimalist culture and way of life and taking on very different experiences such as manufacturing products and pieces for exhibitions, Levy returns to Europe, where he contributes his artistry to another field: contemporary dance and opera by way of set design and installations. The creation of his studio then meant a foray back to art, his first love, followed by industrial design, as well as other ways of expressing his talent.Considering himself now more of a "feeling" artist, Arik Levy continues to contribute substantially to our interior and exterior milieu, his work including public sculpture, as well as complete environments that can be adapted for multi-use. "Life is a system of signs and symbols," he says, "where nothing is quite as it seems."

Burkhard Vogtherr

Burkhard Vogtherr was born in Germany, where he served an apprenticeship as a cabinet maker. He then studied at the School of Industrial Design in Kassel and Wuppertal, graduating in 1967. He started working as a freelance designer In 1972.

At his design studio in Mulhouse, France, he and his team of designers develop new models for clients such as Fritz Hansen, Rolf Benz, Cappellini, Davis Furniture USA, Klöber, and Arflex. In 2000, Burkhard Vogtherr designed the Frame Chair for Arco, which was a major success. This was followed by intensive collaboration. He has won various awards for his designs.

More detailed information about Burkhard Vogtherr can be found at www.vogtherrdesign.com

—

Jonathan Prestwich

After gaining a university degree in industrial design in 1995, Jonathan left England and went to work in a design studio in Boston, Massachusetts, in the United States. He spent the next two years working on lighting and seating, and, while gaining valuable experience, he also became very excited about the design process.

Jonathan then moved to France and spent the next six years working for Burkhard Vogtherr. This was initially intended to be a brief experience but the more Jonathan and Burkhard worked together, the more their working relationship deepened. During this period, which was much like a traditional apprenticeship, Jonathan learned firsthand from a highly respected designer.

In 2003 Jonathan moved back to London. He worked with Barber Osgerby for a brief period then set up his own studio in 2004. Working with other designers was an excellent way to gain experience of how a product is developed and knowledge of what makes a product a good product. Jonathan combines this experience with his desire to design products for the next generation of users, fusing influences from nature and modern technology, with the aim of creating simple, elegant, and innovative products. Since setting up his own studio, Jonathan has worked with several highly respected international companies that produce innovative products and has won numerous awards for his designs, including the Wallpaper Magazine Award, the Interior Innovation Award, and the highly prestigious iF Gold Award.

Burkhard Vogtherr, a celebrated name in the world of furniture design, was born in Germany and began his journey as a cabinet maker apprentice. After honing his skills, he pursued formal education at the prestigious School of Industrial Design in Kassel and Wuppertal, graduating in 1967. Vogtherr embarked on his career as a freelance designer in 1972, establishing himself as a significant figure in the design community.

At his design studio in Mulhouse, France, Burkhard Vogtherr and his talented team have created innovative models for esteemed clients such as Fritz Hansen, Rolf Benz, Cappellini, Davis Furniture USA, Klöber, and Arflex. Among his notable achievements is the Frame Chair designed for Arco in 2000, which marked a significant milestone in his career and led to a fruitful collaboration with the brand.

Vogtherr's work has been widely recognized and awarded, starting with the German Gute Form (Good Design) Prize in 1969. His designs have not only garnered numerous awards but have also left an indelible mark on the international design scene.

After graduating from the prestigious Royal College of Art, Jonas Trampedach (b. 1974) established his Copenhagen-based design studio in 2010, focusing on products, furniture, and lighting.

His studio is renowned for its diverse approach to materials, processes, and techniques, producing works that range from serial production to unique, one-of-a-kind pieces.

Jonas Trampedach's designs are characterized by clear, legible principles and often exhibit a sculptural quality. While his projects are predominantly categorized as industrial design, many incorporate elements of traditional craft, reflecting a seamless blend of modernity and heritage.

Trampedach's design philosophy is deeply rooted in his extensive historical knowledge, which he began cultivating at a young age while working as an international antique dealer. This unique background has enriched his understanding of design and informed his creative process.

He further honed his skills at Danmarks Design Skole before completing a master's degree in Design Products at the Royal College of Art, where he studied under renowned figures such as Ron Arad, Luke Pearson, and Michael Marriot.

Since establishing his studio in Copenhagen, Jonas Trampedach has continued to push the boundaries of product and furniture design, creating pieces that are not only functional but also aesthetically compelling.

The Viennese designer Kai Stania is characterised by his breadth; his design work includes home and office furniture, outdoor furniture, industrial design, and architecture for exhibition stands. His strength is the perfect symbiosis he is able to create between aesthetics and functionality. An array of international and national awards are evidence of his perfect control over the art of good design. These include the White Star Prize, the Red Dot Awards, and the IF-Product Design Award. Kai Stania was born in 1965 in Salzburg and completed his study of architecture at the Vienna University of Technology in 1990 with honours. He also studied product design in Ron Arad’s master class at the University of Applied Arts Vienna. Stania has worked in furniture design for TEAM 7 and Wittmann, and he has designed accessories for Cerruti, Ungaro, and Cacharel. Stania has been significantly involved in the design and development of most product lines at Bene since 1996. In 2002, the architect and designer established his own design studio, Kai Stania I Product Design. Stania designed, among other things, the AL-Management series for Bene, which was featured in the James Bond film “Quantum of Solace.”. AL stands out for its prominent use of aluminium as a symbol of effortlessness and high-tech character. Items in the AL series are also distinguished by their clarity and the way they are pared down to the bare essentials, as well as by their exceptional details and superior quality of construction.

Formed by Gaia Giotti, Giona Scarselli, Cristina Razzanelli, and Erica De Candido, the e-ggs studio has been working in design since 2013 with a cross-disciplinary approach, spanning product design, interior design, and architecture. As architects and designers, they gained professional experience in different areas of design before starting their collaboration. Each project is unique, but the goal is always to design simple, intuitive, and timeless products. Since 2018, they have also been teaching product design at IED Florence.

Willem van Ast grew up in the beautiful Achterhoek region of the Netherlands. As the son of a furniture manufacturer, he felt an affinity for the art of furniture making and production from a young age. After completing his technical training at the Wood and Furniture College in Rotterdam, he started working for Arco, where he was responsible for the collection and was actively involved in the design of new products.

Arco products have won the Red Dot Award, the iF Award for Good Industrial Design, the ION Good Design Award, and the Dutch Design Award in the Interior Product category. Willem van Ast has also won several awards for his designs.

Yonoh’s work has distinguished itself over the years by the progressive improvement of its personal voice. They have rarely flirted excessively with an aesthetic trend. Over time, Yonoh has made its way with fine and elegant work, resolved flawlessly. And they have achieved this by working for companies from other circuits that are less interested in spreading and more interested in contributing, achieving a perfect balance between their own voice and the style of the firm. Many are from the Mediterranean area. Others are from more northern latitudes. And it is not surprising because the main features that, in general, could be assigned to their style have a lot of contemporary Scandinavian character: elegance, warmth, harmony, functionality, and a refined design. But, at the same time, it’s a style with an important amount of Mediterranean code due to its meticulous details, color, and ingenuity. A very Yonoh fusion. As seen as a whole, the work that they’ve done so far reflects a very personal character and path, which shows Clara and Álex’s determination to be a studio with an honest stamp of their own.

Jonathan Prestwich is an accomplished industrial designer who embarked on his career after graduating from University in 1995. His journey began in a design studio in Boston, Massachusetts, where he honed his skills over two years focusing on lighting and seating. This early experience ignited Jonathan's passion for the design process, laying a solid foundation for his future endeavors.

Following his time in the US, Jonathan relocated to France in 1997 to work alongside renowned designer Burkhard Vogtherr. Initially intended as a brief stint, Jonathan's collaboration with Vogtherr evolved into a six-year partnership akin to a traditional apprenticeship. Under Vogtherr's mentorship, Jonathan gained invaluable insights and expertise that would shape his design philosophy and approach.

In 2003, Jonathan made London his home base after a brief tenure with Barber Osgerby. By 2004, he established his design studio, drawing from his extensive collaborative experiences. His tenure collaborating with diverse talents deepened his understanding of product integration and also refined his ability to discern exceptional design.

Jonathan integrates his cumulative experience into his studio's ethos, focusing on creating innovative products for the next generation. His designs seamlessly blend influences from nature and cutting-edge technology, embodying simplicity and elegance. Jonathan's commitment to crafting enduring, user-centric solutions is evident in every project, making his studio a beacon of contemporary design excellence.

Jorre van Ast, born in 1980, is a distinguished figure in the realm of industrial product design. He commenced his journey by studying Industrial Product Design at The Hague University of Applied Sciences, followed by a pivotal two-year tenure at the Flex industrial design studio in the Netherlands. His pursuit of excellence led him to the Royal College of Art in London, where he completed the Design Products course in 2006.

Post-RCA, Van Ast ventured into independent product design at the OKAY studio, a dynamic collective based in North London. Founded on the ethos of collaborative creativity, OKAY studio provided an incubator for innovative ideas and mutual skill enhancement among its members.

In 2007, Jorre Van Ast assumed leadership of the design and development studio at Arco. His appointment as Creative Director in 2009 and subsequent role as company director from 2011 underscore his instrumental impact on the brand. Today, he continues to steer Arco with a distinctive vision, preserving its cherished style while infusing it with his unique creative touch.

Under Van Ast's stewardship, Arco's furniture collection has evolved, maintaining iconic designs while embracing innovations and sustainable practices. His unwavering commitment to excellence and craftsmanship ensures that each piece meets the highest standards of quality and artistry in furniture making.

Since graduating in 2007, Oscar Tange and Panos Vasiliou's recognition in the design world has been on the rise, with exhibitions in London, Tokyo, and Milan showcasing their work. Their designs captured the attention of the international press and well-known manufacturers such as Cappellini, TemaHome, and Levantina, which led them to the commission for the design of the central bar at 100% Design London 2008. Furthermore, they were shortlisted as the Best New British Brand by the Elle Decoration Design Awards 2008, and their design work has been acknowledged by numerous design competitions.In 2009, Panos Vasiliou and Oscar Tange moved to Barcelona, an up-and-coming design capital, where they opened a new design practice called Lime Studio Design. Here, in this inspirational city, Lime Studio Design continues to extend their own design collection by observing, imagining, and exploring. Since moving to Barcelona, Lime Studio Design has been very active, winning design competitions, being commissioned to design a bar in Greece, and exhibiting in Salone Satellite in 2010. Their work was highly profiled, resulting in their products being reviewed by several manufacturing companies. In 2011, Lime Studio Design launched two products: the "Thesi dining chair," which was exhibited in Salone Satellite the previous year, & the Lime tables by Swedese. During 2011, the design studio worked closely with an American company, leading to the marketing of three new products for 2012.

Gudmundur Ludvik Gretarsson, born in 1970 in Reykjavik, Iceland, has carved out a distinguished career at the intersection of craftsmanship, art, and design. His journey from Iceland to Denmark has shaped his unique approach to design, deeply rooted in the Scandinavian tradition of craftsmanship.

Gudmundur's early years in Iceland sparked a curiosity about the composition of objects, leading him to train as a carpenter. After several years in construction, he expanded his horizons by studying sculpture at the Icelandic Academy of Arts. This blend of hands-on craftsmanship and artistic exploration laid a solid foundation for his future endeavors.

In 1999, Gudmundur moved to Copenhagen to pursue furniture design at the Royal Danish Academy. This move proved transformative, allowing him to merge technical precision with aesthetic innovation. He completed his formal education in 2002, earning recognition as a skilled furniture designer.

Gudmundur's career is characterized by a seamless integration of technical expertise and artistic vision. His deep appreciation for wood and traditional craftsmanship informs his work, which balances simplicity with complexity. He views challenges from multiple perspectives, striving to distill his designs to their essence, resulting in simple and intuitive solutions.

In 2010, Lucie set out on her successful path with the distinguished glass lighting collections Muffins and Balloons, which she created for Brokis, a traditional Czech manufacturer. She spent several years in Paris, where she transformed and honed the influences, inspiration, and motives arising from the city’s diverse cultures into her charismatic, elegant style and sensual formal language. Currently based in Prague, she continues working for international clients, creating furniture pieces, glass sculptures, timeless lights, and objects of desire—chic and purified. Her work encompasses daily products, poetic gallery objects, and limited series. Her creative interest mainly focuses on glass and lighting design, involving ever more novel and exciting themes as well as technological and formal solutions in the field. In 2014, Lucie was appointed art director of Brokis, and she has since produced a range of successful designs for the company. Lucie uses classic craftsmanship and cutting-edge technologies, with a primary focus on the pure beauty of materials such as wood and glass. Her emotive style is based on the breathtaking use of colours, unusual proportions, and vibrant formal contrasts, and her objects often overcome the limits of the possible, which is made immediately clear in viewing her work, such as Capsula or the stylish imposing lights Whistle and Mona with dimmable tubular LED light sources developed by Brokis.

The multi-culti designer Tom Kelley was born in East Germany; after heavy problems with the system there and their consequences (2,5 years in Stasi-Prison), he came in the mid-70´ies in the west. I studied in Aachen, Munich, and Long Beach, California. Lives since the end -90´ies in Venezia, Italy; in the beginning, simply coincident, just to be near the Italian clients. Later, companies from Germany, Austria, Czech Republic, Japan, the USA, Holland, Turkey, Indonesia, etc. brought him around the world, sharpening his eyes for the essential and important things. His eastern background somehow provides the capability to find solutions, even in tough situations, finding compromises that lead to the final points. All this is valid as well for all the houses, shops, stands for fairs, etc. that he designed through the years. Just like the reconstruction of the famous Michael Thonet Villa in Bystrice pod Hostynem and the planning of a chair museum in the neighbourhood.

Angelo Mangiarotti stands as one of the great names in Twentieth-century Italian design, celebrated for his commitment, cultivation, and brilliance.

A designer capable of embracing the ideals of the Modern Movement, Mangiarotti went beyond them with his experimental and highly original designs. His unique talent has graced projects of various scales, ranging from architecture to product design.

An advocate of rigorous functionalism, Mangiarotti never neglected the pursuit of elegance and beauty in his work. He consistently pushed beyond conventional boundaries, extending his creativity into sculpture with skillful reflections on plasticity and form. His use of contemporary materials and production processes set him apart, showcasing his innovative spirit.

Mangiarotti's approach and underlying principles reflect a profound awareness of the importance of values with an ethical dimension. He masterfully reconciled the dualism of ethics and aesthetics, creating his own ‘happiness’ through the ‘correctness’ of his practice.

His work remains a testament to the harmonious blend of functionality and beauty, securing his legacy as a luminary in the world of design.

Jan te Lintelo (1954) grew up surrounded by beautiful furniture in his parents’ furniture shop, igniting a lifelong passion for craftsmanship and design from an early age. His formal education in furniture manufacturing in Rotterdam refined his innate creative skills, which he further honed at various renowned brands.

In 1994, Jan embarked on his entrepreneurial journey, establishing Linteloo. His first design, the ‘Easy Living’ sofa, remains a staple in the Linteloo collection, exemplifying the brand's philosophy of timeless, relaxed, and comfortable design. This enduring success speaks to Jan's unique vision and the exceptional quality of his work.

Recently, Jan has entrusted the day-to-day operations to others, allowing him to concentrate fully on product development. His dedication to innovation and design continues, as he frequently returns to the drawing board to create new masterpieces. Linteloo, under Jan's visionary guidance, remains a beacon of elegant and enduring furniture design.

Born in 1979 in Merano (Italy), Alexander Gufler started his career as a goldsmith in his father's shop. Following his degree as a master craftsman in Pforzheim (Germany), he went on to study industrial design at the University of Applied Arts in Vienna, where he graduated in 2009. It was there that he cultivated his passion for furniture and everyday objects.

In 2010, he founded his studio in the heart of Vienna, from which he collaborates with national and international clients. Alexander Gufler creates design objects with a high attention to detail, simplicity, and a profound knowledge of materials and production processes. His work has been exhibited in various countries and honored with international design awards. From 2020 to 2023, Alexander Gufler was responsible for the creative direction of the Czech brand TON.

He studied design at the School of Industrial Design at Lund University in Sweden and then pursued sculpture at the Faculty of Fine Arts at Brno University of Technology. This combination gave him a unique opportunity to perceive design not only from its functional, economic, or production principles but also to look for internal content, individuality, or insight.

Emiliano Salci and Britt Moran pooled the skills they had acquired in their careers and worked in design, the arts, and fashion, and in 2003 they founded DIMORESTUDIO.

From that moment on, each project has been conceived and developed in a personal, recognisable way. Objects by Masters of Design co-exist with salvaged items and others custom-designed to create a specific atmosphere.

A timeless visual and emotive course. Research is so rich in details as to make every sign, every idea, every suggestion, innovation, and reference simple and, ipso facto, sophisticated.

A language formed by an emotive chemistry of errors, reutilizations, inventions, enhancements of prints, lights, lacquering, and oxidations. A censureless aesthetic wherein each selected element has expressive energy and citizenship because it is a feature of a whole. DIMORESTUDIO interprets memories and makes dreams a reality. DIMORESTUDIO works in the fields of design and the arts, architecture, and fashion.

Piero Lissoni is architect, art director and designer and is recognized as one of the masters of contemporary design.

For over thirty years, he has developed projects the world over in the fields of architecture, landscape and interior, as well as in product and graphic design. He is also art director for Alpi, B&B Italia, Boffi, Living Divani, Lema, Lualdi, Porro and Sanlorenzo.

Some of the most important architecture and interior projects deserving of mention include the Dorothea Hotel in Budapest (2023), the AKA hotel in New York (2023), the Shangri-La Shougang Park in Beijing (2021) realized for the 2022 winter Olympics, The Ritz-Carlton Residences in Miami Beach (2020), the Grand Park Hotel in Rovigno, Croatia (2019), the restyling of the historic Camparino in Galleria Bar in Milan (2019), The Middle House for the Swire Hotels Group in Shanghai (2018), the residential projects One Paraiso and Grand Paraiso in Miami (2018), The Oberoi Al Zorah Beach Resort Ajman in the UAE (2017), the interiors of a series of yachts for Sanlorenzo (2017-2020), the Conservatorium Hotel in Amsterdam (2012), the renovation of the historic Teatro Nazionale in Milan (2009), in addition to the corporate headquarters of Boffi, Glas Italia, Fantini, Living Divani and the Sanlorenzo shipyards in La Spezia and Ameglia.

Piero Lissoni has received a series of international awards, including the Good Design Award, the Red Dot Award and the Compasso d’Oro ADI.

Peter Fehrentz, a multi-disciplinary figure involved in furniture design, interior photography, and interior design, brings a unique perspective to every project he undertakes.

His creative journey began with a profound realization: "I design because it binds my love," a sentiment that guided his work since his formative years studying product and metal design in Hildesheim in 1992.

Fehrentz's approach to design transcends mere functionality; it explores the nuanced interplay between objects and their environments. This fascination led him from product design to diverse roles in styling, set design, and photography, enriching his understanding of spatial dynamics and aesthetic harmony.

Today, Peter Fehrentz integrates his extensive expertise into collaborative ventures with leading brands in the interior and design sectors. His portfolio reflects a seamless fusion of disciplines, where each project evolves into a visual narrative, blending new colors, forms, and materials to create unexpected and captivating spaces.

Driven by a passion for authenticity, Fehrentz finds inspiration in businesses and individuals who share his commitment to innovation and quality. His intuitive, playful approach to design is marked by moments where creativity flows effortlessly, resulting in distinctive and energized outcomes.

Sjoerd Vroonland (1985) studied at the Wood and Furniture College in Rotterdam and also graduated from the ArtEZ Academy, specializing in product design. Today, Vroonland runs his own label from his atelier in Amsterdam.

His innovative products are inspired by classic designs and are created using materials, techniques, and shapes that we are all familiar with. From the archetypal saddle to the antique clothespin, Vroonland uses the best features of time-honored design and finds new ways to apply them, combining contrasting materials.

Instead of hiding all the ‘technical’ details, the designer focuses on the beauty of structure and construction: the materials are uncompromised and the line of work can be clearly seen. The result is a pure and poetic dialogue between craft, innovation, and modern-day living.

Florence native Isabella Sodi began her extensive career in the fields of fashion and design as an interior designer for exhibitions, homes, and shops. She moved to Milan to to focus on fashion design and then to Rome, where she worked passionately on haute couture. She has since returned to Milan, where she now lives, and is much appreciated in the field of furnishings and interiors.

French designer, born in Paris, Inga Sempé graduated in 1993 at the Ensci Atelier (Ecole Nationale Supérieure de Création Industrielle, Paris), She has worked in several design and interior architecture studios. In 2000, she opened her own atelier in Paris, where she lives. From 2000 to 2001, in Villa Medici in Rome (Academie de France), she has developed her first personal projects, which were then produced by Edra and Cappellini. In 2011, she was awarded with the EDIDA (Elle Decor International Award). The following year, she was a guest of honour at Stockholm furniture fair.
The designer collaborates with the French brand Ligne Roset and with international companies such as LucePlan, Wästberg, Moustache, Alessi, Roros, Hay, Svenkst Tenn and Cassina. Recently, she has won “Form + 1” at Stockholm furniture fair for the clamp lamp “W153,” edited by the swedish company Wästberg.

VISSERMEIJWAARD is a design studio focused on product and scenographic exhibition design based in Arnhem, The Netherlands. Creative director Vera Meijwaard, born in 1988 in Naarden, The Netherlands, studied product design at ArtEZ University of the Arts in Arnhem. By playing with materiality, context, and scale and with a fascination for ‘form follows function’, she researches new applications for hidden aesthetics found in the everyday. With an associative working method, she creates distinct but minimalistic designs from an aesthetic world of material, color, and shape.

The studio’s wide range of work extends from furniture and lighting to textile, ceramic, and glassware. VISSERMEIJWAARD collaborates with, among others, design brands Linteloo, Moooi Carpets, Ames and Pulpo Products. Meijwaard is also known for creating unique sets and window designs that she creates for the French fashion house Hermès. Work has been presented in museums, galleries, and fairs worldwide, including London, Paris, Milan, New York, Tokyo, Hong Kong, Barcelona, Cologne and Copenhagen.

Guillaume Lopez (1989) began his design journey under the mentorship of renowned designers François Bauchet and Éric Jourdan at the prestigious Saint-Étienne School of Art and Design. His early career was profoundly shaped by his collaboration with Christophe Delcourt, a leading brand where he honed his passion for elegant finishes and refined color palettes.

This robust foundation led Guillaume to Toronto, where he joined the multidisciplinary design studio Yabu Pushelberg. Under the guidance of George Yabu and Glenn Pushelberg, he contributed to exclusive projects worldwide, further refining his skills and expanding his creative horizons.

In 2022, Guillaume embarked on a new chapter, establishing his own studio in the picturesque French Alps. Here, he develops innovative creations, blending his extensive experience with fresh inspirations. His work combines state-of-the-art manufacturing techniques with natural materials, showcasing a unique design philosophy rooted in the power of collaboration and shared passion.

For Guillaume, design transcends the creation of aesthetically pleasing objects; it is an engaging dialogue with forms, materials, and craftsmanship. His distinctive creations emerge from these harmonious interactions, reflecting his sensitivity while evoking emotion and amazement.

This official collaboration between Yael Mer & Shay Alkalay started after many years of sharing life, thoughts, and ideas. Yael’s main focus includes turning two-dimensional sheet materials into curvaceous functional forms, whereas Shay is fascinated by how things move, function, and react.Since their graduation show at the Royal College of Art in 2006, they have received several highly respected awards, including the British Council Talented Award, the iF Gold Award, the Dutch Design Award, the Wallpaper* Design Award 2009, the Elle Decoration International Design Award for best furniture of 2008–09, and the Designer of the Future Award 2009 from Design Miami/Basel. Their works have been exhibited internationally and can be found within the permanent collections of the MoMA New York and other museums. They, for clients including Cappellini, Established & Sons, Moroso, Kvadrat, Stella McCartney, Arco, Mutina, Lema, and, in addition, Yael & Shay, produce from time to time unique and limited-edition designs and installations within their own studio in London. Their work has been featured in many major design publications and newspapers worldwide.

Anthony Guerrée, a French designer born in Normandy in 1987, lives and works in Paris, where he has established a reputation for blending high-quality craftsmanship with the rich history of French decorative arts.

Educated at the prestigious École Boulle in Paris, a venerable institution since 1886, Guerrée honed his skills in fine and applied arts, with a strong emphasis on craftsmanship. His work is characterized by its functional yet bold, sculptural forms and meticulous detailing.

Guerrée's career took a significant turn in 2010 when he joined Studio Andrée Putman. There, he collaborated with world-class artisans to create exquisite interiors. In 2015, he furthered his experience by working with designer Christophe Delcourt, contributing to the Delcourt Collection and various collaborations with renowned French and international brands.

In 2020, Guerrée launched his independent design studio, developing a unique signature style influenced by his travels, personal interactions, and literary inspirations. His debut furniture collection, "Chairs of Lost Time," draws inspiration from Marcel Proust’s novel "À la recherche du temps perdu," with each sculptural chair representing a character from the novel. This collection encapsulates Guerrée's design philosophy: “I love to design a collection of furniture as one that tells a story because each design has to be more than simply functional.”

In 2022, Guerrée's innovative "Verdurin" screen chair was inducted into the collections of Mobilier National, and he was honored with the “French Design 100” prize at the Palais de l’Élysée by the Presidency of the French Republic.

Anthony Guerrée collaborates with numerous influential French and international brands and galleries in the furniture and lifestyle sectors, including Linteloo and De La Espada.

Architect and designer India Mahdavi is based in Paris. Her studio, created in 2000, is known for the diversity of its international projects, which explore the fields of architecture, interior design, scenography, furniture, and object design.She likes to define herself as polychrome and polyglot. Settled on one single street in Paris, rue Las Cases, India Mahdavi has created a very unique environment, combining a modern sense of comfort and elegance with color and humour. A cross-cultural art de vivre.

Sabine Marcelis is a designer living and working in Rotterdam, the Netherlands. Raised in New Zealand, she studied industrial design for two years at Victoria University of Wellington and continued her studies at the Design Academy Eindhoven, where she graduated in 2011. Since graduating, she has been operating Studio Sabine Marcelis, working within the fields of product, installation, and spatial design with a strong focus on materiality. Her work is characterized by pure forms, which highlight material properties.

Marcelis applies a strong aesthetic point of view to her collaborations with industry specialists. This method of working allows her to intervene in the manufacturing process, using material research and experimentation to achieve new and surprising visual effects for projects both showcased in galleries and commissioned by commercial clients.

She sees her designs as experiences and strives to create a dialogue between the object and the user. The aesthetic core of her work is informed by the function of the selected materials. Since starting her studio, Marcelis’ work has been exhibited at the Museum Boijmans van Beuningen, Rotterdam, and the Musee des Arts Decoratifs, Paris. Her custom project client list includes Céline, Isabel Marant, Aesop, Burberry, and Salle Privée.

Gabriele Buratti, architect and former professor of Interior Design at the Faculty of Design of Politecnico di Milano (2003-2010), and Oscar Buratti, architect, are the co-founders of Buratti Architetti. Based on a foundation of rich academic experience and a deep understanding of design principles, their studio excels in diverse architectural and design projects.

Buratti Architetti engages in a wide spectrum of endeavors, from the architecture of buildings and urban spaces to the intricate interiors of residential homes and commercial establishments. Their expertise extends to furniture and lighting design and creating compelling exhibition and showroom environments.

This breadth of practice reflects a dynamic and comprehensive professional journey, where themes, environments, brands, and craftsmanship converge to inspire innovative solutions.

Specializing in residential, commercial, and industrial constructions, the studio undertakes interventions in historic districts, complex building renovations, and the meticulous design of villas and office interiors. Their approach merges cultural insight with specialized proficiency, blending experimental concepts with technical precision to deliver projects of distinctive vision and superior quality.

Buratti Architetti boasts extensive collaborations with leading furniture and retail brands, underscoring their influence and versatility in shaping contemporary environments.

Through their dedication to excellence and holistic design philosophy, Oscar e Gabriele Buratti continues to redefine spatial experiences with every project they undertake.

The works of the designer Werner Aisslinger cover a spectrum of experimental and artistic approaches, including product design and architecture.
He delights in making use of the latest technologies and has helped introduce new materials and techniques to the world of design. His visionary products and spaces explore civilization-related and futuristic topics. Werner Aisslinger´s “loft cube project became one of the most discussed modular and transportable housing projects within the last few years. His “hemp chair from 2012, developed with BASF and Moroso, was the worldwide first biocomposite and compostable monobloc chair made of natural fibers. His work is exhibited in the permanent collections of international museums such as the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) and the Metropolitan Museum in New York, the French Fonds National d´Art Contemporain in Paris, the Victora & Albert Museum London or the Museum Neue Sammlung, the Design Museum in Munich, and the Vitra Design Museum Collection in Weil, Germany. In 2013, Werner Aisslinger opened his first large solo show called “Home of the Future” in Berlin’s Museum, “Haus am Waldsee.”. In 2014, he was awarded the prestigious AW Designer of the Year Award in Cologne. In 2017, he opened his exhibition “House of Wonders “ in the Design Museum of the Pinakothek der Moderne in Munich. Among Studio Aisslinger's interior architecture projects, his hotels for the 25 Hours Hotel Group and mall designs for the Central Group in Bangkok, New Work projects for Metropolis in San Francisco and Houston or office landscapes for Zalando in Germany are known for their innovative storytelling and spatial conceptual approach. He works for brands like Vitra, Moroso, Nomos, Cappellini, Dedon, Accor, Magis, Hansgrohe Axor, Kaldewei, Foscarini & Waestberg. Werner Aisslinger lives and works in Berlin, with a second studio in Singapore.

Belgium-based, signature designer Sylvain Willenz has been creating furniture, lighting, and products for international clients since 2004.

Born in Brussels in 1978, Sylvain Willenz has lived consecutively in the US, Belgium, and the UK. Sylvain studied MA in Design Products at the Royal College of Art in London, graduating in 2003. In 2004, he opened his design office, based in Brussels, Belgium. Considered one of the most influential Belgian creatives, Sylvain was named Belgian Designer of the Year in 2009.

Renowned for his strong yet simple graphic aesthetic, Sylvain is fascinated by production, and manufacturing and pays great attention to detail. Originally wanting to be a cartoonist, Sylvain turned towards product design. Over the years, Sylvain developed a singular style of ligne claire, which instills his simple, elegant, and graphic designs, which are so recognizable and unique today.

Designs by Sylvain Willenz have been granted many awards, including the iF Awards and the Red Dot Best of the Best Awards. In 2009, the TORCH light collection was nominated for Best in Lighting and Overall Best Product of the Year at the UK Grand Designs Awards. Considered one of the most influential Belgian creatives, Sylvain was named Belgian Designer of the Year in 2009.

Kateřina Handlová is a graduate of the Atelier of Glass at AAAD Prague (Academy of Arts Prague). She combines glass with different materials, such as leather, ropes, or porcelain, to create objects that mediate her personal experiences as well as her fascination with the world of haute couture. Kateřina finds beauty in quality craftsmanship, which explains her passion for detail. She loves glass for its unique character, problems, and advantages. With her original artistic approach, she is definitely one of the most gifted and remarkable young Czech glass designers.

After studying at the Glass school in Nový Bor, Ota Svoboda was part of the design duo Born in Bor. In 2013 Ota Svoboda joined Bomma. Being extremely skilled and gifted he soon became the head of product development and main internal driver of new design developments. He participated in the technological progress of every collection. He combines his creative vision with glass craftsmanship and technological know-how.

Rony Plesl, designer of the collection, explains, “The Metamorphosis lighting collection was created in collaboration with the Rückl Glassworks. The shapes, colors, and particularly the hand-cut pattern carry the most iconic features of this brand: precision, sharpness, and the geometry of each cut. Combined with light, they create an outstanding experience. Each individual component is a unique stand-alone piece, resembling the fruit of a tree that enhances any composition. The BOMMA Constellation by Rückl is a true rarity in the world of lighting.”

Jan Plecháč and Henry Wielgus met while studying at AAAD Prague (the Academy of Arts Prague). Jan went on to exhibit his thesis project at Milan’s Salone Satellite 2011, gaining much attention in the industry. He was awarded the Elle Décor International Award and Discovery of the Year at the Czech Grand Design Awards. In 2012, Jan and Henry founded Studio Jan Plechac & Henry Wielgus. Their work transcends the barriers between art, architecture, and design. Their clients include Rossana Orlandi Gallery Milan, Mint Shop London, Luminaire, Lasvit, Cappellini, or Menu.

DECHEM studio was established in Prague in 2012 by Michaela Tomiskova and Jakub Jandourek. They first met at the Novy Bor School of Glass, where they both fell in love with the earliest synthesised material in human history. While Michaela went on to study product design at AAAD Prague (Academy of Arts Prague) and later worked for several acclaimed design studios, Jakub continued to hone his skills in the lighting industry, specialising in glass workmanship, technology, and manufacturing. Since establishing DECHEM Studio, they have focused on product design and paid special attention to glass.

The studio was founded in 1990 during the Vitra Design Museum workshop led by Denis Santachiara. At that time, Michal Froněk and Jan Němeček studied architecture at AAAD Prague (Academy of Arts Prague). Jan had a car and could drive them to the workshop in Switzerland, the start of a 25-year collaboration. They focus on industrial design, interiors, and the reconstruction of historical and industrial buildings. Professor Michal and Jan now head the Atelier of Design at AAAD Prague. They inspire and influence the youngest generation of Czech talents. They have become ambassadors of Czech design. Their works are represented in the collections of prestigious galleries and museums, such as the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York.

Boris Klimek graduated with studies in product and conceptual design from the Academy of Art, Architecture, and Design in Prague. During his studies, he attended internships in England (Sheffield Hallam University), Slovakia (Academy of Fine Arts and Design), or in product design studios in Prague led by Olgoj Chorchoj designers. Boris has been awarded a number of awards for his product design. In his work, he tries to find certain conceptual overlaps, games, and stories. He tries to make his products not only functional but mostly poetic, playful, and able to stimulate the human imagination and emotions.

Václav and Jakub are part of a notable wave of young designers with international experience. Both graduated from UMPRUM in Prague and the Royal College of Art in London. They worked together between 2011 and 2020 at Studio deFORM, creating some of Bomma’s most successful collections. Now they each run their own studios. Since 2017, Václav Mlynář has been the creative director of Bomma.

BIG-GAME is a Swiss-based design studio founded in 2004 by Augustin Scott de Martinville, Elric Petit, and Grégoire Jeanmonod. We created the blimp collection with the idea of a massive cloud. this giant crystal cloud seemingly floats in space and balances on the edge of both technical and human possibility

Since 2017, Václav Mlynář has been the creative director of BOMMA. He has international experience from studying and working in the Czech Republic, the US, Denmark, and London. He obtained his master degrees in product design at the Royal College of Art in London and the Academy of Arts, Architecture, and Design in Prague. In 2011–2020, Václav was running, with Jakub Pollág, the award-winning product design studio deFORM. In 2020, Václav focused on running his product studios, VM, spatial/interior architecture studio, and MONUMENT Office.

Takashi Shinozaki, BA, MA, architect and designer, was born in 1968 in Tokyo, where he currently lives and works. After having studied in Japan and Italy, he graduated from the Tokyo National University of the Arts with a Master's degree and worked at an architectural design firm. He established his own practice in 1997 in Tokyo. He works in various fields of design, including private houses, collective housing, offices, restaurants, retail shops, exhibitions, furniture, and graphic design. His works were awarded the Good Design Award in Japan several times and were exhibited at exhibitions in Milan, Seoul, Nagoya, and Tokyo, including two solo exhibitions, “Ueno, Milano, Gotanda” (2006, Tokyo) and “A New Domestic Animal Landscape” (2009, Tokyo). Since 2004, he has been teaching at several schools in Japan.

Giuseppe Bavuso, architect and designer, lives and works in Seregno. In his projects, he combines the rigor of an almost minimalist style with extensive technical research, skillfully using technological and innovative materials. His initial professional experiences saw him involved in designing important projects in various sectors, including architecture, the interior design of large communal spaces, the automotive industry, and the interior design of trains and sleeping cars. Since 1986, at his product design studio, he has been designing bio-medical equipment for companies. At the very beginning of his career, a decisive factor was his encounter with Bruno Munari, a great intellectual with the unparalleled gift of synthesis. This enlightening and fundamental experience profoundly influenced his way of perceiving his career and his approach to design. In the furniture sector, he collaborates with various well-established international companies as a designer and art director, dealing with the entire product cycle, from concept creation to product engineering and communication.

Tomoya Tabuchi, born in 1974 in Tochigi, Japan, graduated in living design from Kuwasawa Design School in 1997. After working at a Japanese furniture firm on planning, designing original products, and developing products designed by overseas designers, he became independent in 2010.

In 2011, the Tee table was debuted by Interiors (a Japanese firm) as his first design after independence.

His activities include designing products, graphic design, and art direction.

He has received Premio Vico Magistretti, shortlist for the prize (2007), ELLE DÉCOR Young Japanese Design Talent (2012), Good Design Award (2014), and Architizer A+Awards (2016).

He is collaborating with Lapalma, Viccarbe, Systemtronic, Interiors, and others.

Born in Modena, degree studied architecture in Florence. In 1985–87, he worked at Cesare Leonardi’s studio. He has designed for: Alessi, Authentics (D), Driade, Elmes (Jp), Lapalma (IT), and Serralunga. 1992/2003 ”Top Ten” award at Promosedia; 1997 “Design Plus” award in Frankfurt; 2001 selection for the Compasso d’Oro award in Milan. “Segreta” stool and ladder by Driade is part of the permanent exhibition at the Victoria and Albert Museum in London. In 2005: personal show “Watercolors Industrial Design” in Jatsuo, Japan.

Hee Welling (born 1974) is educated at the University of Art and Design Helsinki (Finland) and the Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts, department of furniture design, from where he graduated with a master degree in furniture design in 2003.
He established his own studio in Copenhagen in 2003 and works in the fields of furniture, interior, and industrial design.
Among his clients are Hay, Erik Jørgensen, Fredericia, Lammhults, Laufen, and Lapalma.
His work has been awarded several awards, including the BoBedre design award (Denmark), the Good Design award (USA), and the Danish Art Foundation (Denmark). He has been nominated as Danish designer of the year in 2013 and 2014 and is listed on the Architonic Top 200 of popular designers and architects.
Hee Welling’s design has been exhibited at different fairs and museums around the world, e.g., the Danish Export Exhibition in Tokyo, the ICFF in New York, 100% Design in London, the Stockholm Furniture Fair, the IMM in Cologne, Maison & Objet in Paris, and Salone del Mobile in Milan.
His work has been used for different international interior projects, such as the Oslo opera house (Norway), the Australian parliament, Google head office, Beats by Dre head office, Amazon head office, Danish national television, and the Danish parliament.
Besides his studio, he taught at the Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts and the Danish Institute for Study Abroad (DIS).

Shin Azumi was born in Kobe, Japan, in 1965. After finishing his MA at the Royal College of Art in 1994, he started working as a design unit, ‘AZUMI’. In 2005, he established his own office, ‘a studio’ in London, and he moved his base back to Japan in Tokyo in 2016. Currently, he is taking a professorship at Hosei University, faculty of engineering and Design, in Japan. The clients list of ‘a studio’ includes lapalma, Magis, Desalto, Fredericia, Tefal, Authentics and many other international brands. Shin Azumi’s work has received numerous design awards in Europe and in Japan, among them the Blueprint 100% Design Award and “Product of the Year” of FX International Design Award. He also took part in the jury of numerous design awards, such as the IF Design Award (DE). His works were acquired by many international museums, such as Victoria & Albert Museum (UK), Stedelijk Museum (Holland), etc.

ABOUT CHRIS MARTIN

In 1992, 22-year-old Englishman Chris Martin began his career as an assistant to one of the world’s most prominent designers, Jasper Morrison. A few years later, he was presented with a job offer he couldn’t resist. It was the mid-90s and Chris Martin moved to Stockholm to work for the architect Thomas Sandell. His tasks there consisted of designing furniture for various clients and in his new hometown, Chris Martin quickly found a friend in Magnus Elebäck. The friendship grew and in 1999, they decided to start their own business. A wish to work independently from other producers developed gradually, and in 2009 they launched Mass Productions, an operation where they are today in charge of not only design but also manufacturing, distribution, and marketing. Through this all-encompassing approach, they aim to create timeless design through an in-depth knowledge of sustainability, materials, and manufacturing techniques. The breakthrough came with the launch of Tio, a wire chair with a clear identity and visual lightness. Chris Martin has since been honored with numerous awards, including Designer of the Year, Design Profile of the Year, and now the prestigious Bruno Mathsson Prize.

ABOUT THE MATHSSON FOUNDATION

The Bruno Mathsson Prize is handed out by the Karin and Bruno Mathsson Foundation in Sweden, which was created in 1983 through a donation from Karin and Bruno Mathsson. The purpose of the foundation is to support research, development, and education related to objects as well as indoor and outdoor environments.

ABOUT VANDALORUM

Vandalorum is located amidst the fields on the outskirts of Värnamo and presents the most influential art and design today. Vandalorum annually presents 10–15 unique exhibitions featuring prominent contemporary designers and artists, both Swedish and international. The buildings, reminiscent of the monumental barn that once dominated the site, are built according to an original concept by award-winning Italian architect Renzo Piano. The interior was designed by Icelandic architect Sigurdur Gustafsson in consultation with Vandalorum’s founder, Sven Lundh.

Architect and designer, he graduated in ‘91 in Florence, where he resides. In the 90's, he collaborated with architect Claudio Nardi’s studio, designing private residences, public spaces, and boutiques for leading Italian designers like Dolce & Gabbana, G. Ferrè, and Valentino. Since '98, he has been a freelance designer of furniture, furnishing complements, exhibition space, and company image communication. His client list includes Lapalma, RF-Yamakawa, and True Design. For Clerprem, he worked as an art director, overseeing the design of seats and components for trains. He was a teaching assistant to Prof. Robert Segoni in the course “Industrial Design” before becoming a teacher himself, lecturing for the Bachelor of Fashion Design in Florence until ‘09. The subjects he taught included “Furniture Design Product” and “Theory and History of Industrial Design.”He currently teaches history of design at IED and furniture design for the international masters at Florence Institute of Design International. He won the “Good Design Award” at the Chicago Athenaeum in 2013.

Tomoko Azumi ( born in 1966, in Hiroshima, Japan ) is an interior and furniture designer based in London. She studied architecture and interior design at Kyoto City University of Art, worked in an architectural studio in Tokyo, then moved to London in 1992. She finished a master's course in furniture design at the Royal College of Art in 1995. After her activities as a partner at AZUMI (1995–2004), she started her practice at TNA Design Studio in East London in 2005.

At TNA Design Studio, her main fields are furniture, lighting products, interior design, and exhibition space design. She has been collaborating with international clients such as Benchmark Furniture ( UK ), Lapalma ( Italy ), Maxray ( Japan ), Röthlisberger ( Switzerland ), and Swarovski ( Austria ), as well as interior design for the Supreme Court of the United Kingdom, shops for Harrys of London and exhibition designs for the Crafts Council ( UK ).

Her works are in several permanent collections, including the Victoria & Albert Museum, the Geoffrey Museum in London, and the Stedelijk Museum ( Netherlands ). She has been a project teacher at the Design Products of the Royal College of Art since 2005 and a research fellow at London Metropolitan University from 2006 to 2009. Also, she has been a member of the jury for the Good Design Award in Japan since 2006, a member of the selection board for 100% Design London in 2010, and a member of the jury for the Design Award at the Cologne Furniture Fair in 2010 and 2011.

Shin Azumi was born in Kobe, Japan, in 1965. After finishing his MA at the Royal College of Art in 1994, he started working in a design unit, ‘AZUMI’. In 2005, he established his own office, ‘a studio’ in London, and he moved his base back to Japan in Tokyo in 2016. Currently, he is taking a professorship at Hosei University, faculty of engineering and design in Japan. The client list of ‘a studio’ includes Lapalma, Magis, Desalto, Fredericia, Tefal, Authentics, and many other international brands. Shin Azumi’s work has received numerous design awards in Europe and in Japan, among which the Blueprint 100% Design Award and the “Product of the Year” of the FX International Design Award. He also took part in the jury of numerous design awards such as the IF Design Award (DE). His works were acquired by many international museums such as the Victoria & Albert Museum (UK), Stedelijk Museum (Holland), etc.

Mario Ferrarini was born in Como, Italy, in 1978. Graduated in Industrial Design at the Polytechnic of Milan he began practicing as a consultant for a number of renowned design studios until founding his eponymous studio in 2007, working alongside international companies on different projects ranging industrial products supporting leading companies such as Antoniolupi, Atlas Concorde, Bitossi, Castelli, Crassevig, Davis furniture, Desalto, de Sede, Ethimo, Franke, Jacuzzi, La Palma, Ligne Roset, Living Divani, Poltrona Frau, Potocco, Viccarbe, Wendelbo and others. The studio works alongside companies to create unusual products that are timeless and transcend passing trends, targeted finding long-lasting solutions for clients with production aimed towards a large consumer market with recognizable solutions and acclaimed distinguishing features.

The two brothers were born in Venice in 1971 and 1973. They graduated in architecture in Venice in 1998. In 1996, they opened the design studio PIO&TITOTOSO Design. They work in the field of architecture, implementing many projects both nationally and internationally, and participate in various competitions. They also collaborate in the furniture and design world, working with the most prestigious companies, including Artemide, iGuzzini, Foscarini, Leucos, Venini and Vistosi for lighting, and Alessi, Pedrali, Colombo Design, Guzzini, Metalco and Segis for furniture. They design stands, exhibitions, stores, offices, and warehouses for various companies, taking care of their image and graphics.

They have received the following awards: Red Dot Design Award 2017, Index Compasso d'Oro 2016, Design Plus 2016, Selezione al Compasso d'Oro Internazionale 2015, Red Dot Design Award 2015, Red Dot Design Award 2012, Good Design Award 2009, Selezione I.Dot 2006-2007, and Young & Design 2005.

They have also taken part in exhibitions in Europe and the USA.

Anderssen & Voll is an Oslo-based design studio run by Torbjørn Anderssen (1976) and Espen Voll (1965). Torbjørn graduated from the Bergen Academy of Art and Design, and Espen graduated from the Oslo National Academy of the Arts. As cofounders of Norway Says in 2000, they were forerunners in an emerging international movement amongst young Norwegian designers. Since the startup of Anderssen & Voll in 2009, their work has spanned from textile design through tableware, lighting, and furniture to electronics for some of Europe’s most renowned international design brands.

Their collaborations include work for companies like Lapalma, Muuto, Wrong for Hay, Foscarini, Kvadrat, Erik Jørgensen, and Filip Technologies. They also take great pride in working with some of the finest Norwegian traditional industries, like Jøtul, Gudbrandsdalens Uldvarefabrik, Fjord Fiesta, Røros Tweed, and LK Hjelle. Working with the very different industrial cultures spanning from the mountains of Norway to the plains of Veneto is a continuous source of inspiration. Their work has received numerous awards, like the Honorary Award for Design Excellence in Norway, the Wallpaper Award, the IF Award, and the Torsten and Wanja Söderberg Prize.

Design Office Antti Kotilainen was founded in 1997 in Helsinki. Specializing in furniture design, his studio also provides product design as well as complete visual concepts ranging from graphic to spatial design. A characteristic of Kotilainen’s design work is organic functionalism set to meet contemporary qualifications. Behind his work is a drive to refine technical issues and details—of the manufacturing process and its applicability—into the aesthetics of the final product. He aims to compose designs that look as effortless as they look essential. In the best Scandinavian tradition, Kotilainen has explored new ways to work with wood in numerous of his furniture designs. Since 1993, he has collaborated with several Scandinavian furniture manufacturers, for example, Martela, and Piiroinen, and also with Knoll from the United States and Arper from Italy. Kotilainen’s furniture has been widely recognized and his work has been displayed in several Finnish and international expositions. His furniture has been utilized in furnishing public spaces in various companies, universities, and cultural sites in numerous European countries, the US, Australia, and Japan.

Karri Monni, born 1969 in Varkaus, Finland. Furniture carpenter studies 1985-1987. Studies in Lahti Institute of Design, in Department of Interior Architecture and Furniture Design 1994-1998, BA. Interior Architect and Designer, SIO. Practiced in Studio Simo Heikkilä 1997, Industrial Design Ltd, Sistem 1998-1999, Keiski Architects 2000, Pekka Salminen Architects 2000. Started own Studio Karri Monni 1999. Started own company Koon Design Oy 2006. Furniture designs for Lapalma from 2001. As a member of Interior Architects Monni & Koskivaara, furniture design /interior design work for Amer Sports, Nordea Bank Finland, Vacon, Sampo Group, Metso, Leiras Takeda Pharmaceutical, WinWinD, EFG, City of Helsinki, Roche, YLE, Valio, Walki, Aventis Pharma, McKinsey & Company. Karri Monni lives and works in Helsinki.

Romano Marcato was born in 1951, in Cadoneghe, a few kilometres from Padua. In 1970, he obtained a high school diploma from the Istituto Tecnico Industriale of Padua, specializing in mechanical engineering. From 1970 to 1978, he was in charge of design at a leading small metal parts industry and designed high-speed cutting dies for electric terminals.

Thanks to his extensive knowledge of precision engineering and complex high-speed production systems, Romano Marcato designed automatic machines and systems for the automotive and electronics industries. In 1979, with his brother Dario, he founded the company Lapalma, and with his distinct personal flair and expertise in using metals, he designed the first products and oversaw the technical processes.

For many years, Romano Marcato has been choosing designers with whom to collaborate and oversee the development and project engineering of all products, even the most futuristic, thanks to his thirty years of experience and technical knowledge that drives him to customize individual machines.

He has also designed some of the most popular objects in the Lapalma collection, including the BRUNCH and PANCO tables, the WIL stool, the BRIO and YO small tables, and the ARIA chair.

Born in 1950 in Pianiga, near Venice. He still lives and works on the Riviera del Brenta. During his cultural education at the Venice Academy of Fine Arts and at the Industrial Design Course at the Department of Architecture of the University of Venice, he designed and created a lot of art objects, which have been shown in important international exhibitions. Since the 1980s, he has been working full-time in industrial design, collaborating with prestigious furniture brands, from Magis to Artemide, from Lago to Gervasoni.
